name: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc.
summary:
J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT) reported earnings of $1.91 per share on revenue of $3.50 billion for the second quarter ended June 2026. The consensus earnings estimate was $1.71 per share on revenue of $3.17 billion. The Earnings Whisper number was $1.75 per share. The company beat expectations by 9.14% while revenue grew 19.37% on a year-over-year basis.

“I’m grateful for our people and their continued focus on delivering operational excellence around service, safety and cost discipline in this dynamic environment,” said Shelley Simpson, president and CEO. “Our second quarter results reflect the strength of executing our strategy, as we leveraged our investments in our people, technology, and capacity to drive growth and improve profitability. As market conditions continue to evolve, we remain focused on creating long-term value for our shareholders, delivering valuable solutions to our customers while maintaining discipline around returns on our capital.”

JB Hunt Transport Services Inc, together with its wholly owned subsidiaries provides transportation and delivery services to a group of customers and consumers throughout the continental United States, Canada and Mexico.
subject: J.B. Hunt Transport Services Beat Expectations

name: United Airlines
summary:
United Airlines (UAL) reported earnings of $1.99 per share on revenue of $17.67 billion for the second quarter ended June 2026. The consensus earnings estimate was $1.92 per share on revenue of $17.68 billion. The Earnings Whisper number was $2.00 per share. The company missed expectations by 0.50% while revenue grew 15.99% on a year-over-year basis.

The company said it expects 2026 earnings of $9.00 to $11.00 per share. The company's previous guidance was earnings of $7.00 to $11.00 per share, and the current consensus earnings estimate is $10.50 per share for the year ending December 31, 2026.

"Our results show why we have been investing in customer improvements throughout every cabin and winning brand-loyal customers," said United CEO Scott Kirby. "United is built to thrive in every environment, and when oil prices spiked in March, we quickly and decisively acted to adjust our schedules, while simultaneously doubling down on our customer investments. Our brand-loyal customers value their travel on United whether they are in Polaris or in Economy. Our network expansions, investment in Starlink, and innovations such as Relax Row are giving customers new reasons to choose United."

United Continental Holdings Inc transports people and cargo through its mainline operations, which use jet aircraft with at least 118 seats, and its regional operations.
subject: United Airlines Missed Expectations

subject: Plumas Bancorp Beat Consensus Estimates

name: Cintas Corporation
summary:

The consensus earnings estimate was $1.24 per share on revenue of $2.88 billion. The Earnings Whisper number was $1.25 per share. The company said it expects fiscal 2027 earnings of $5.36 to $5.50 per share on revenue of $12.10 billion to $12.25 billion. The current consensus earnings estimate is $5.42 per share on revenue of $12.07 billion for the year ending May 31, 2027.

Todd M. Schneider, Cintas’ President and Chief Executive Officer, stated “These results conclude an outstanding year for Cintas. We delivered record revenues and operating margins. Our 8.3% organic revenue growth demonstrates our ability to deliver strong results in a dynamic macro environment. Our all-time high gross margin reflects the outstanding performance of our employee-partners and the clear impact of our investments in technology, capacity and talent. These results continue to showcase the strength and resilience of Cintas' value proposition."

Cintas Corp is a provider of corporate identity uniforms. It operates in four segments: Rental Uniforms & Ancillary Products, Uniform Direct Sales, First Aid, Safety & Fire Protection Services, & Document Management Services.
subject: Cintas Beat Expectations

Bill Demchak, PNC Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, said, “Our strong second quarter performance reflects the disciplined execution of our growth strategy and positions us well for the second half of the year. We remain focused on delivering results that support our customers, shareholders and communities. Our successful FirstBank conversion, strong capital position and announced dividend increase provide further evidence of the strength and resilience of our franchise.”

PNC Financial Services Group Inc is a financial services company. It operates in six segments: Retail Banking; Corporate & Institutional Banking; Asset Management Group; Residential Mortgage Banking; BlackRock; and Non-Strategic Assets Portfolio.

name: Johnson & Johnson
summary:
Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) reported earnings of $2.90 per share on revenue of $25.31 billion for the second quarter ended June 2026. The consensus earnings estimate was $2.84 per share on revenue of $25.04 billion. The Earnings Whisper number was $2.86 per share. The company beat expectations by 1.40% while revenue grew 6.60% on a year-over-year basis.

The company said it expects 2026 earnings of $11.60 to $11.75 per share on revenue of $100.30 billion to $100.90 billion. The company's previous guidance was earnings of $11.45 to $11.65 per share on revenue of $100.30 billion to $101.30 billion, and the current consensus earnings estimate is $11.58 per share on revenue of $100.81 billion for the year ending December 31, 2026.

“Johnson & Johnson delivered strong second-quarter results, demonstrating the power of our innovation, the depth of our portfolio and the momentum in our pipeline as we advance transformative treatments that address the world’s toughest health challenges,” said Joaquin Duato, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, Johnson & Johnson. “With raised guidance and quarterly sales surpassing $25 billion, we are on track to meet our 2026 target of more than $100 billion in annual revenue for the first time in our Company’s 140-year history.”

Johnson & Johnson is a holding company, which is engaged in the research and development, manufacture and sale of products in the health care field within its Consumer, Pharmaceutical and Medical Devices, and Diagnostics business segments.
subject: Johnson & Johnson Beat Expectations

name: Elevance Health
summary:
Elevance Health (ELV) reported earnings of $7.45 per share on revenue of $50.47 billion for the second quarter ended June 2026. The consensus earnings estimate was $6.18 per share on revenue of $48.45 billion. The Earnings Whisper number was $6.43 per share. The company beat expectations by 15.86% while revenue grew 1.40% on a year-over-year basis.

The company said it expects 2026 earnings of at least $27.00 per share. The company's previous guidance was earnings of at least $26.75 per share, andt the current consensus earnings estimate is $26.86 per share for the year ending December 31, 2026.

Gail K. Boudreaux, President and Chief Executive Officer, said, "Our second quarter results exceeded our outlook, supported by disciplined execution and improved operating performance across our diversified portfolio. We are raising our 2026 adjusted EPS guidance to at least $27.00 and accelerating targeted investments in the capabilities that matter most: medical cost management, member experience, provider connectivity, operating efficiency, and Carelon’s value-based solutions. These actions will strengthen how we operate, improve consistency over time, and reinforce our confidence in returning to at least 12% adjusted EPS growth in 2027 off our 2026 earnings baseline.”

Elevance Health is a lifetime, trusted health partner fueled by its purpose to improve the health of humanity.
subject: Elevance Health Beat Expectations

name: ASML Holding
summary:
ASML (ASML) reported earnings of $8.81 per share on revenue of $10.84 billion for the second quarter ended June 2026. The consensus earnings estimate was $7.98 per share on revenue of $10.28 billion. The Earnings Whisper number was $8.32 per share. The company beat expectations by 5.89% while revenue grew 24.24% on a year-over-year basis.

The company said it expects third-quarter revenue of $12.56 billion to $13.70 billion and now expects 2026 revenue of $49.10 billion to $51.38 billion. The company's previous guidance was 2026 revenue of $42.4 billion to $47.1 billion. The current consensus revenue estimate is $11.34 billion for the quarter ending September 30, 2026 and revenue of $45.35 billion for the year ending December 31, 2026.

ASML President and Chief Executive Officer Christophe Fouquet said, "Our second-quarter total net sales were €9.3 billion and gross margin came in at 54.0%, both above guidance, driven primarily by higher than expected Installed Base Management sales. Ongoing AI-related investments and continued progress in AI technologies are driving demand for advanced Logic and Memory chips, further strengthening the semiconductor industry's growth outlook. Our customers, in turn, continue to accelerate their capacity expansion plans."

ASML Holding NV is a provider of lithography systems for the semiconductor industry, manufactures complex machines that are critical to the production of integrated circuits or chips. Its products include TWINSCAN, PAS 5500, Optics, among others.
subject: ASML Beat Expectations

subject: Equity Bancshares Beat Consensus Estimates

The company said it expects fiscal 2026 revenue of $1.020 billion to $1.025 billion. The company's previous guidance was revenue of $1.025 billion to $1.035 billion, and the current consensus revenue estimate is $1.03 billion for the year ending August 31, 2026.

subject: Phoenix Education Partners, Inc. Beat Consensus Estimates

name: Aehr Test Systems
summary:
Aehr Test Systems (AEHR) reported earnings of $0.11 per share on revenue of $18.84 million for the fiscal fourth quarter ended May 2026. The consensus estimate was a loss of $0.01 per share on revenue of $18.70 million. The Earnings Whisper number was $0.02 per share. The company beat expectations by 450.00% while revenue grew 33.69% on a year-over-year basis.

The company said it expects fiscal 2027 revenue of $130.0 million to $150.0 million. The current consensus estimate is revenue of $86.6 million for the year ending May 31, 2027.

Gayn Erickson, President and CEO of Aehr Test Systems, commented: "We are very pleased with our fiscal fourth quarter performance, which exceeded expectations and capped a year of significant bookings and revenue diversification for Aehr. Record quarterly bookings, a very strong backlog, and growing demand across AI processors, silicon photonics, and power semiconductors for our wafer-level and package-level burn-in solutions position us well for significant growth moving forward."

Aehr Test Systems is a worldwide provider of test systems for burning-in and testing logic, optical and memory integrated circuits and has installed over 2,500 systems worldwide.
subject: Aehr Test Systems Beat Expectations

subject: Kestra Medical Technologies, Missed Consensus Estimates

name: Citigroup, Inc.
summary:
Citigroup (C) reported earnings of $3.15 per share on revenue of $45.30 billion for the second quarter ended June 2026. The consensus earnings estimate was $2.72 per share on revenue of $23.68 billion. The Earnings Whisper number was $2.79 per share. The company beat expectations by 12.90% while revenue grew 6.97% on a year-over-year basis.

The company said in its earnings presentation it continues to expect 2026 NII, ex-Markets, of $89.5 billion to $90.3 billion. The current consensus revenue estimate is $94.0 billion for the year ending December 31, 2026.

Citi Chair and CEO Jane Fraser said, “With net income up 45%, this was Citi’s best quarterly revenue in a decade with double-digit revenue growth for the firm and in four out of our five businesses. Services delivered its highest ever quarterly revenue and a return of over 30%. In Markets, strong results in FX and spread products also show how much clients rely on our global network while Equities showed continued momentum with revenues up 45%. Banking revenues climbed 34% and we played a role in the majority of the top equity and debt issuances. Wealth revenues increased for the 9th straight quarter with almost two-thirds of Net New Investment Assets growth coming from deepening relationships with existing clients. Despite short-term headwinds from investments in our U.S. Consumer Cards portfolio, our resilient customer base kept fueling underlying drivers: loan growth, higher spend and better credit performance than expected."

Citi is a preeminent banking partner for institutions with cross-border needs, a global leader in wealth management and a valued personal bank in its home market of the United States.
subject: Citigroup Beat Expectations

name: Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.
summary:
Goldman Sachs Group (GS) reported earnings of $20.98 per share on revenue of $38.43 billion for the second quarter ended June 2026. The consensus earnings estimate was $14.47 per share on revenue of $16.49 billion. The Earnings Whisper number was $15.21 per share. The company beat expectations by 37.94% while revenue grew 22.91% on a year-over-year basis.

David Solomon, Chairman and CEO of Goldman Sachs, said, “Our record performance this quarter reflects the strength of our global franchise, the depth of our relationships, and our ability to harness the power of One Goldman Sachs. Momentum has accelerated throughout our businesses. Clients are turning to us to lead their most strategic and consequential transactions, which are often the genesis of activity across the franchise. We are relentlessly driving our long-term growth strategy across Global Banking & Markets and Asset & Wealth Management, and given what we see in our pipelines, we expect this flywheel of activity to continue.”

Goldman Sachs Group Inc is a investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Its segments include Investment Banking, Trading and Principal Investments, Asset Management and Securities Services.
subject: Goldman Sachs Group Beat Expectations

subject: Fastenal Missed Expectations

name: Wells Fargo & Co.
summary:
Wells Fargo (WFC) reported earnings of $1.96 per share on revenue of $33.59 billion for the second quarter ended June 2026. The consensus earnings estimate was $1.73 per share on revenue of $21.80 billion. The Earnings Whisper number was $1.76 per share. The company beat expectations by 11.36% while revenue grew 10.37% on a year-over-year basis.

Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Charlie Scharf commented, “We generated strong second quarter results with diluted earnings per share growing to $2.00, up 25% from a year ago. Revenue increased 9% from a year ago with net interest income up 5% and noninterest income up 13%. Continued expense discipline drove positive operating leverage. Total expenses were up 2% from a year ago and non-revenue related expenses were lower. Consumer and commercial credit quality remained strong across all portfolios and net loan charge-offs declined 10 basis points from a year ago."

Wells Fargo & Co is a diversified financial services company. It provides retail, corporate and commercial banking services through banking stores and offices, the internet and other distribution channels to individuals, businesses and institutions.
subject: Wells Fargo Beat Expectations

name: Bank of America Corp.
summary:
Bank of America (BAC) reported earnings of $1.21 per share on revenue of $49.39 billion for the second quarter ended June 2026. The consensus earnings estimate was $1.13 per share on revenue of $30.62 billion. The Earnings Whisper number was $1.15 per share. The company beat expectations by 5.22% while revenue grew 5.84% on a year-over-year basis.

Chair and CEO Brian Moynihan, said, "The team delivered one of our strongest quarters to date, with earnings per share up 34% year-over-year. Every business segment reported double digit net income growth and strong returns on equity. Revenue increased 15% from last year as we deepened relationships with existing clients and welcomed new ones. Against a healthy economic backdrop, resilient consumers and businesses are turning to Bank of America to spend, borrow and invest. It was also an exceptional quarter for our markets-facing businesses, with investment banking fees up 50% year-over-year. Near-term, pipelines remain strong, and commercial borrowing has picked up. "

Bank of America Corporation is a bank holding and a financial holding company. Through its subsidiaries, it provides banking and non-banking financial services and products throughout the United States and in selected international markets.
subject: Bank of America Beat Expectations

name: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
summary:
JPMorgan Chase (JPM) reported earnings of $6.14 per share on revenue of $82.46 billion for the second quarter ended June 2026. The consensus earnings estimate was $5.59 per share on revenue of $48.71 billion. The Earnings Whisper number was $5.61 per share. The company beat expectations by 9.45% while revenue grew 17.89% on a year-over-year basis.

Jamie Dimon, Chairman and CEO, commented on the financial results: “The Firm reported very strong results in the quarter, generating net income of $16.9 billion and an ROTCE of 23%, excluding gains related to Visa and certain equity investments. These results were the product of a particularly favorable environment with an elevated level of market activity, as well as rigorous execution, years of consistent investment and thoughtful capital deployment.”

JPMorgan Chase & Co is a financial services firm and a banking institution. its segments are Consumer & Community Banking, Corporate & Investment Bank, Commercial Banking, and Asset Management.
subject: JPMorgan Chase Beat Expectations

AngioDynamics Inc designs, manufactures and sells medical, surgical and diagnostic devices used by professional healthcare providers for vascular access, for the treatment of peripheral vascular disease and for use in oncology and surgical settings.
